annotations.label: Hypercomplex number

anchor.snippet: is a traditional term for an element of a finite-dimensional unital algebra

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes finite-dimensional unital associative algebras via `Algebra` plus `Module.Finite` but has no dedicated 'hypercomplex number' notion.

annotations.label: Hurwitz's theorem

anchor.snippet: Hurwitz's theorem says finite-dimensional real composition algebras are the reals

annotations.note: Mathlib has no composition-algebra structure, so Hurwitz's classification is absent.

annotations.label: Frobenius theorem

anchor.snippet: the Frobenius theorem says the only real associative division algebras are

annotations.note: The only 'Frobenius' results in Mathlib concern the characteristic-p endomorphism, not the classification of real associative division algebras.

annotations.label: Adams generalization (Hopf invariants)

anchor.snippet: published a further generalization in terms of Hopf invariants on H -spaces that still limits the dimension to 1, 2, 4, or 8

annotations.note: Adams' Hopf-invariant-one dimension restriction is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Matrices isomorphic to coquaternions

anchor.snippet: real matrices were found isomorphic to coquaternions

annotations.note: No coquaternion/split-quaternion type or its matrix isomorphism appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Wedderburn's matrix representation

anchor.snippet: associative hypercomplex systems could be represented by square matrices

annotations.note: The Artin–Wedderburn theorem expresses semisimple rings as products of matrix rings over division rings, covering this matrix-representation idea more generally.

annotations.label: Hypercomplex number (Kantor & Solodovnikov)

anchor.snippet: given by Kantor & Solodovnikov (1989) as an element of a unital

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Algebra` typeclass captures unital algebras but does not define the specific Kantor–Solodovnikov hypercomplex notion.

annotations.label: Classification of 2-dimensional unital real algebras

anchor.snippet: there are exactly three 2-dimensional unital algebras over the reals

annotations.note: No classification theorem of 2-dimensional unital real algebras exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Complex numbers the only quadratic field

anchor.snippet: The complex numbers are the only quadratic algebra that is a field

annotations.note: This characterization among 2-dimensional real algebras is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Split algebras are not division algebras

anchor.snippet: so such algebras cannot be division algebras

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define split algebras nor prove they fail to be division algebras.

annotations.label: Clifford algebra

anchor.snippet: A Clifford algebra is the unital associative algebra generated over an underlying vector space

annotations.note: `CliffordAlgebra Q` is defined as the quotient of the tensor algebra by `ι m * ι m = Q m`, matching the definition exactly.

anchors.snippet: A Clifford algebra is the unital associative algebra generated over an underlying vector space

annotations.label: Clifford algebra label notation

anchor.snippet: the remaining Clifford algebras can be identified by the label

annotations.note: Clifford algebras are parameterized by an arbitrary quadratic form, but Mathlib has no Cl_{p,q} signature-label notation.

annotations.label: Geometric algebras

anchor.snippet: These algebras, called geometric algebras , form a systematic set

annotations.note: Geometric algebras are real Clifford algebras, which `CliffordAlgebra` subsumes, though the term itself is not used.

annotations.label: Examples of Clifford algebras

anchor.snippet: Examples include: the complex numbers

annotations.note: Equivs.lean realizes the complex numbers, quaternions, and dual numbers as specific Clifford algebras via `CliffordAlgebraComplex.equiv`, `CliffordAlgebraQuaternion.equiv`, and `CliffordAlgebraDualNumber.equiv`.

annotations.label: Even subalgebra parametrises rotations

anchor.snippet: form an even subalgebra

annotations.note: The even subalgebra `CliffordAlgebra.even Q` and its universal property are formalized, but its role as a parametrization of rotations is not stated as such.

annotations.label: Clifford algebras retain associativity

anchor.snippet: Clifford algebras retain associativity at any number of dimensions

annotations.note: `CliffordAlgebra Q` carries a `Ring` instance for any quadratic form, so associativity holds in all dimensions by invoking `mul_assoc`.

annotations.label: Porteous Proposition 11.4 (recognition of subalgebras)

anchor.snippet: be a real associative algebra with unit element

annotations.note: Porteous's subalgebra-recognition criterion is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Clifford algebras with square +1 elements not division algebras

anchor.snippet: contain non-real elements that square to +1

annotations.note: Mathlib does not prove that Clifford algebras containing square-+1 elements fail to be division algebras.

annotations.label: Cayley–Dickson construction

anchor.snippet: where all the non-real basis elements anti-commute and satisfy

annotations.note: The Cayley–Dickson doubling construction is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Non-associativity in 8+ dimensions

anchor.snippet: these algebras are non-associative

annotations.note: Without octonions or the Cayley–Dickson construction, this non-associativity result is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Zero divisors in 16+ dimensions

anchor.snippet: these algebras also have nonzero zero-divisors

annotations.note: Sedenions and their zero divisors are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quaternions, octonions, sedenions

anchor.snippet: The first algebras in this sequence include the 4-dimensional quaternions

annotations.note: Quaternions are formalized as `Quaternion`/`QuaternionAlgebra`, but octonions and sedenions are not present.

annotations.label: Algebraic symmetry lost with dimension

anchor.snippet: An algebraic symmetry is lost with each increase in dimensionality

annotations.note: This qualitative statement about the Cayley–Dickson tower is not formalized.

annotations.label: Modified construction generates split algebras

anchor.snippet: can be modified by inserting an extra sign at some stages

annotations.note: The sign-modified Cayley–Dickson construction producing split algebras is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Split-complex numbers

anchor.snippet: split-complex numbers with basis

annotations.note: Mathlib has no split-complex number type.

annotations.label: Split-quaternions

anchor.snippet: split-quaternions with basis

annotations.note: Split-quaternions arise as the special case `QuaternionAlgebra ℝ 1 0 1` of Mathlib's general quaternion algebra, but are not defined by name.

annotations.label: Split-octonions

anchor.snippet: split-octonions with basis

annotations.note: No octonions, split or otherwise, exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Properties of split-complex numbers

anchor.snippet: the split-complex numbers are not algebraically closed

annotations.note: Without a split-complex type, these properties are not formalized.

annotations.label: Properties of split-quaternions

anchor.snippet: split-quaternions are not commutative, but further contain nilpotents

annotations.note: The specific nilpotent/non-commutativity properties of split-quaternions are not proven in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Properties of split-octonions

anchor.snippet: Split-octonions are non-associative and contain nilpotents

annotations.note: Split-octonions and their properties are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tensor product of algebras is an algebra

anchor.snippet: The tensor product of any two algebras is another algebra

annotations.note: Mathlib provides `Algebra R (A ⊗[R] B)`, giving the tensor product of two R-algebras its algebra structure.

annotations.label: Tensor products with the complex numbers

anchor.snippet: taking tensor products with the complex numbers

annotations.note: Complexifications like ℂ ⊗ A are instances of the general tensor-product algebra, but the specific classifications listed are not stated.

annotations.label: Tensor product of two quaternion algebras

anchor.snippet: Another example is the tensor product of two quaternion algebras

annotations.note: The tensor product ℍ ⊗ ℍ is an algebra via the general instance, but its isomorphism to a 4×4 matrix algebra is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hyperquaternion algebras

anchor.snippet: (and its subalgebras) as

annotations.note: Mathlib has no 'hyperquaternion' algebra notion despite having the building-block tensor products and quaternion algebras.

annotations.label: Bicomplex numbers

anchor.snippet: a 4-dimensional vector space over the reals, 2-dimensional over the complex numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib's 'bicomplex' occurrences are homological bicomplexes, not bicomplex numbers.

annotations.label: Multicomplex numbers

anchor.snippet: vector spaces over the reals

annotations.note: Multicomplex numbers are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Composition algebra

anchor.snippet: algebra with a quadratic form that composes with the product

annotations.note: Mathlib has `QuadraticForm` but no composition-algebra structure with a multiplicative norm form.
